threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when a bus screams past you on Mare Street, like you've never seen traffic move with such chaos. Welcome to Hackney!
Caught a night bus from Brixton, and the driver played old reggae tracks. Even the grumpiest passengers couldn't resist tapping their feet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One night, I heard a saxophone playing 'Careless Whisper' while a cat strutted down the street like it owned the place. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find spontaneous street parties where the crowd sings along to techno tracks blaring from a random balcony at dawn.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can predict the exact moment BART's going to get stuck again, without even checking the app.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver singing to a group of tourists while waiting at the stop. Even the guy in a suit was cracking up. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into a bar and get a drink immediately. In Gràcia, you’ll often wait for your order while everyone enjoys the conversation first.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle to find the right 'cerveseria'; they ask for 'cerveza' and get a look from the locals.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look startled when the Tube's delayed—locals just grab a coffee and roll with it.
Caught a random busker on the 45 last night, he played ‘Wonderwall’ on a ukulele, and somehow it turned into a singalong. Never seen so many smiles at 3 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I stumbled upon a street art piece of a cat wearing a beret, holding a coffee cup. Classic Neukölln, blending the absurd with the everyday.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous block parties where everyone brings a dish to share, and the kids kick a ball in the street without a care.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they roll their eyes at tourists waiting for BART to announce a delay. It's like, welcome to the club, buddy.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus speeding past a guy juggling flaming torches at Eddy & Jones. He didn’t miss a beat when the driver honked. Classic Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but it’s the hidden squares and local bars where the real soul of the neighborhood hangs out.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still struggle with the 'll' sound in 'Barcelona'. That awkward 'y' slip gives it away every time.
